title: Bank of England enhances resolution readiness with updated operational guides
summary: The Bank of England published new and updated guidance on how the Bank might implement the UK resolution regime in the event of a bank failure, enhancing resolution readiness for systemically important institutions.

title: Franklin Templeton launches enhanced indexing equity fund range for UK investors
summary: Franklin Templeton has launched four enhanced indexing equity funds for UK investors, combining factor-based investing with a conviction signal derived from its fundamental managers. The range targets UK advisers and wealth managers.

title: FTSE 100 falls as Iran conflict uncertainty lifts oil, hits risk appetite
summary: The FTSE 100 closed down 17 points at 10,582 as oil surged following the breakdown of US-Iran peace talks. Airlines, builders and banks led falls. BP and Shell gained on higher energy prices. Brent crude jumped 7% to just under $102 a barrel.

title: UK gilt yields surge as geopolitical risks fuel inflation fears
summary: The yield on the UK 10-year gilt climbed to 4.8%, nearing multi-year highs, as inflation fears reignited after the collapse of US-Iran peace talks. Traders are pricing in nearly two Bank of England rate hikes by the end of 2026.

title: Equity fund outflows surge to 1.44bn during war-torn March
summary: Outflows from equity funds leapt to 1.44bn in March from Februarys 927m, according to Calastones latest Fund Flow Index. The significant increase in outflows was driven by geopolitical tensions and the Iran conflict.

title: FTSE 100 jumps nearly 3% to one-month high on Iran ceasefire relief
summary: The FTSE 100 surged more than 300 points, a gain of approximately 2.5-3.1%, to trade at its highest level since early March on Iran ceasefire relief. Risk appetite returned across European equity markets.

title: S&P 500 technical outlook April 2026: navigating the moving average resistance
summary: The S&P 500 consensus price target among Wall Street strategists is 5,400-5,600. Multinational giants within the S&P 500 see a boost from international revenue streams, now accounting for 42% of total index sales.

title: UK government borrowing costs hit highest level since 2008 as inflation fears hit gilt market
summary: British government borrowing costs surged to their highest since the 2008 financial crisis, with the benchmark 10-year rate crossing 5% as investors scrambled to price in rising inflation risks and growing probability of interest rate hikes.

title: UK property market update: April 2026 — mortgage rates surge, mixed signals
summary: The average two-year fixed mortgage rate surged from 4.83% on 2 March to 5.9% on 8 April, its highest level since July 2024. Average UK house price stands at approximately 270,500. Under offers rose 8.1% annually.

title: LTAF AUM surges to 7.3bn as ISA changes take effect
summary: Long-term asset fund AUM surged to 7.3bn as new ISA rules took effect, opening up private markets investments to a broader range of retail investors through the innovative fund structure.

title: FTSE 100 wipes out 2026 gains amid Iran conflict and rising borrowing costs
summary: The FTSE 100 wiped out all 2026 gains amid the Iran conflict and rising borrowing costs. Higher energy costs could push inflation back above 3%, squeezing consumer incomes. Diesel prices topped 2 per litre.

title: ONS: GDP quarterly national accounts Q4 2025 — growth at 0.1%
summary: Real gross domestic product increased by 0.1% in Q4 Oct to Dec 2025 and 1% annually. The quarterly national accounts provide a comprehensive picture of UK economic activity including household spending and business investment.

title: Zoopla house price index: March 2026 — prices rise 2.2% annually
summary: Nationwide reports annual house price growth picking up to 2.2% in March, with the average UK property valued at 277,186. Northern Ireland was the standout performer with Halifax recording annual growth of 8.7%.

title: ONS: Consumer price inflation UK February 2026 — CPI at 3.0%
summary: The Consumer Prices Index CPI rose by 3.0% in the 12 months to February 2026, unchanged from January. CPIH rose by 3.2%. Food and non-alcoholic beverages prices rose 3.3%. Services inflation at 4.3%, lowest since March 2022.

title: Advisers pivot to annuities and bonds ahead of IHT reform
summary: Financial advisers are increasingly recommending annuities and bonds to clients ahead of inheritance tax reforms taking effect from April 2027. The pivot reflects changing retirement income strategies as pension IHT rules shift.

title: The morning briefing: Aberdeen leans on ii as adviser profits fall; IHT change may trigger DC exodus
summary: The decision in the Autumn Budget 2024 to bring unspent pension pots into scope of inheritance tax is already changing behaviour among those with larger defined contribution savings. The Government estimates 10,000 estates brought into IHT.

title: Investment Week reveals finalists for Fund Manager of the Year Awards 2026
summary: Investment Week announced the finalists for its Fund Manager of the Year Awards 2026, recognising excellence across multiple fund categories including equity, fixed income, multi-asset and alternatives.

title: FCA conduct breach reports rise 10%; IPTF brings in new adviser cohort
summary: FCA conduct breach reports rose 10% year-on-year. The Intelligence Platform Task Force brought in a new adviser cohort and board member as the regulator continues to strengthen its supervisory approach.

title: ONS: Private rents increase 3.5% annually to 1,374 average in February 2026
summary: Average UK monthly private rents increased by 3.5% to 1,374 in the 12 months to February 2026. Rents increased to 1,430 (3.6%) in England, 828 (5.5%) in Wales, and 1,022 (2.4%) in Scotland.

title: Bank of England agents summary of business conditions — March 2026
summary: Wage settlements are expected to average 3.6% in 2026, with contacts reporting lower pay pressures than last year. Intelligence points to consumer price inflation continuing to ease despite geopolitical headwinds.

title: Bank Rate maintained at 3.75% — March 2026 monetary policy summary and minutes
summary: The Monetary Policy Committee voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 3.75%. Conflict in the Middle East caused a significant increase in global energy and commodity prices. CPI inflation expected to rise to 3.5% over coming quarters.

title: Adviser demand surges for flexible IHT solutions
summary: Adviser demand has surged for flexible inheritance tax solutions as clients prepare for reforms. Ongoing market volatility adds complexity to long-term planning and increases the likelihood of client hesitation.

title: State pension set for 4.7% rise in April 2026 amid triple-lock pressures
summary: The state pension is set for a 4.7% rise in April 2026 as the triple-lock mechanism continues to drive above-inflation increases. The rise reflects the higher of average earnings growth, CPI inflation, or 2.5%.

title: The morning briefing: Perspective acquires six more firms; adviser conversations drive surge in charitable giving
summary: Perspective Financial Group acquired six more financial planning firms in Q1 2026, adding 825m in assets under management. Adviser conversations are driving a surge in charitable giving as clients seek tax-efficient strategies.

title: Men in UK financial services earn 40,000 more than female colleagues
summary: Female financial services professionals in the UK earn nearly 40,000 less than men on average. The average base salary for men in UK financial services is 128,407 compared to 99,053 for women, a gap of roughly 30%.

title: ONS: Construction output falls 2.0% in three months to January 2026
summary: Construction output fell by 2.0% in the three months to January 2026, with private housing new work falling by 6.3%. The decline reflects weakening residential construction activity amid higher mortgage rates and uncertain demand.

title: Investment Week Fixed Income Market Focus: Iran crisis and geopolitical tensions reshape outlook
summary: Investment Weeks Fixed Income Market Focus explored how different parts of the fixed income market have been impacted by the Iran crisis and escalating global geopolitical tensions. Fund managers reflected on outlooks for inflation and rates.

title: ONS: GDP monthly estimate UK January 2026 — economy grows 0.2%
summary: The economy grew by 0.2% in the three months to January 2026. Monthly GDP provides a timely estimate of economic growth, with services, production, and construction sectors all contributing to the headline figure.

title: Bank of England streamlines reporting and disclosure requirements for bank failure regime
summary: The threshold for firms in scope of Resolution Assessment Framework reporting will increase from 50bn to 100bn in retail deposits. Small Domestic Deposit Takers will review recovery plans every two years rather than annually.

title: MPs debate pension IHT reforms as Finance Bill reaches third reading
summary: The Finance Bill implements tax measures announced in the Autumn Budget, including new dividend tax rates from April 2026. Pension IHT legislation described as massively complex, with individuals urged to seek financial advice.
